topic: Religious & Ethical Teachings about Crime & Punishment
subject: religious-studies

items.correct: Five distinct arguments for God's existence: from motion, causation, contingency, gradation of being, and final causes/teleology — collectively forming Aquinas's natural theology, not a single argument.
items.misconception: Aquinas's Five Ways are five steps of one cosmological argument.
items.random_wrong: Aquinas's Five Ways are five Christian sacraments.
items.why: Each Way is a self-standing argument with different structure. Students treat them as a single chain, missing that Aquinas offered multiple independent routes — the failure of one doesn't undermine the others.

items.q: What do Christians mean by resurrection, and how does it differ from reincarnation?
items.correct: Resurrection is the once-for-all bodily raising to eternal life at the end of time (modelled on Christ's resurrection); reincarnation is the soul's repeated rebirth into new bodies. They are distinct doctrines from different religious traditions.
items.misconception: Resurrection and reincarnation are basically the same idea of life after death.
items.random_wrong: Christians believe everyone is reincarnated until they accept Jesus.
items.why: Christianity affirms one bodily resurrection, not cyclical rebirth. Students flatten different afterlife concepts into a single 'life after death' idea, missing the very different metaphysics involved.

items.correct: The first five books of the Hebrew Bible (Genesis, Exodus, Leviticus, Numbers, Deuteronomy) — traditionally attributed to Moses and containing law, narrative and covenant.
items.misconception: The Torah is the same as the Book of Genesis.
items.random_wrong: The Torah is a single scroll containing every Jewish text.
items.why: The Torah is five books, not one. Wider Jewish scripture (Tanakh) also includes Nevi'im (Prophets) and Ketuvim (Writings). Students often equate Torah with Genesis or with the whole Hebrew Bible.

items.q: When was UK capital punishment abolished?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1965. “1948” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In Matthew 18:22, Jesus tells Peter to forgive 'seventy times seven'. What number is seven multiplied by seventy?
items.why: Exact answer: 490 times. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
